figures.caption: Figure 3: Examples of the medical report for the (a) SKINCON, (b) WBCAtt, (c) CBIS, and (d) MIMIC-CXR datasets.

Table 1: Multiple-choice VQA accuracy on the SKINCON, WBCAtt, and CBIS datasets. We compared our method (InstFree) with BLIP-2 [39], MedGemma-4B [24], PubMedVision-7B [15], Qwen2.5-VL-3B [23], and LLaMA-3.2-11B-Vision [22]. FT denotes fine-tuning, and RS denotes response shuffling. Bold indicates the highest accuracy, and underline denotes the second highest.

Figure 2: An illustration of our instruction-free tuning framework. The vision encoder g extracts key-value matrices KV v from an image Xv, which are then integrated into a prompt Xp for the language model f to generate the response ŷ. For instructionfree tuning, the text instruction in Xp is replaced with the momentum proxy instruction t̄. During supervised fine-tuning, g is updated with autoregressive loss L between the response ŷ and the ground truth y. In parallel, (warm-up initialized) t̄ is gradually updated via exponential moving average of the proxy instruction t. During inference, t̄ is discarded, and a conversational text instruction (e.g., “Describe...”) is used to generate a natural language response (e.g., “The image depicts...”).

Figure 1: Conceptual illustrations of (a) instruction tuning, which fine-tunes the model on curated instructions and outputs, and (b) instruction-free tuning, which fine-tunes the model solely on paired textual descriptions (e.g., radiology reports). Instruction tuning requires instruction-image-output triplets constructed by humans or LLMs prior to fine-tuning, whereas instruction-free tuning can be performed on image-description pairs without additional steps. Examples of an image, an instruction-output pair for instruction tuning, and a description used for instruction-free tuning are shown in (c).

Figure 8: Qualitative VQA results on the WBCAtt dataset. Correct responses are marked in green, incorrect responses are marked in red, and responses that do not follow instructions are marked in orange.
Figure 4: Examples of multiple-choice VQA for the (a) SKINCON, (b) WBCAtt, (c) CBIS, and (d) MIMIC-CXR datasets.

Figure 5: Radar charts of the accuracy for each attribute (or question type) across the (a) WBCAtt, (b) CBIS, and (c) MIMIC-CXR datasets. LLaMA-3.2-11B-Vision (w/o FT) is shown in black, LLaMA-3.2-11B-Vision (FT) in green, InstFree in blue, InstFree w/ RS in red, and MedGemma-4B in yellow-green.
